title: Olotrema oligocarpa
taxonomy: Olotrema oligocarpa is a species of fungus in the family Gomphillaceae. It belongs to the class Dothideomycetes, a group of fungi characterized by their ascospores and perithecia. The genus Olotrema is part of the order Gomphales, which includes fungi that typically produce cup-shaped fruiting bodies. Olotrema oligocarpa is classified as a lichenous fungus, growing in association with a photosynthetic partner. This classification is based on its morphology and reproductive structures.
uses: Olotrema oligocarpa has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant is sometimes used to treat gastrointestinal problems and skin infections. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and benefits.

The health benefits of Olotrema oligocarpa include its potential to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of this plant include various compounds such as usnic acid, barbatic acid, and lecanoric acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
